candidates.stmt:
I’m standing to be your county councillor because I love our area – its community, heritage, and natural beauty – and I’m committed to making it sustainable, safe, and thriving for future generations. That’s why I proudly wear the Green rosette: the party shares my commitment to local health and welfare but allows me to always put you first – not a party manifesto.

I believe being your representative shouldn’t just be about relaying questions to council officers. Your issues are my issues, and I won’t stop until I have clear reasons, solutions, and a plan to bring them sooner. And I’m not afraid to stand up publicly for your causes. Many in Brampton saw this when, after residents expressed frustration at last November’s chaotic multi-agency flood meeting, I stepped up as impromptu chair, ensuring we got clear answers. I’ve since helped found Brampton’s Flood Local Action Group.

I’ve supported Grafham and Brampton residents in keeping Wibbly Wobbly Road open, securing official confirmation it will remain open to all vehicles with improved safety. And inspired by Buckden Parish Council’s fantastic youth initiatives, I’ve agreed to help Brampton and Grafham PCs set up their own youth councils too.

But having worked closely with existing local councillors, I know all this is just the tip of the iceberg. A vote for me is a vote for someone who will champion a sustainable, safe, and thriving Cambridgeshire we can all be proud to call home.

Committed to fostering social cohesion, sustainability, better travel and personal safety. Joy has established a women’s hub in Sawtry to strengthen community engagement and well-being. As a change professional, she has a proven track record of delivering strategic initiatives and is dedicated to driving positive change in Sawtry & Stilton.

Having previous experience as a City Councillor, I have a track record of making tangible improvements to play areas, parks and green spaces and pushing the Labour-run council to improve its ambition, especially on climate change and biodiversity.

In my professional life, I am a hospital doctor and researcher, as well as a Trade Union representative defending NHS staff from unfair conditions. Since moving to West Chesterton, I have helped make sure pavements are safe and clear for pedestrians; worked to remove bollards which are obstructive to prams and wheelchairs. When previously a councillor, I spoke up for residents on many occasions at council, holding the Labour-run administration to account across key issues for our city; whether it be water, housing, the wider environment or ecology. I’m proud of my work as a community campaigner and hope that you will vote for more hard work in May.

I would like to be considered as a district councillor for the Buckden Ward because Buckden is our home, and we have grown attached to the village and people who make Buckden such a special place to live.

Although I am new to local government, I have gained crucial transferable skills as a retired civil servant with a thorough understanding of working collaboratively across the private sector,  government departments at home and abroad including: The MOD, FCDO, and UKAid. I have led capacity building projects with domestic and foreign police and defence services to establish post conflict security and governance infrastructures within fragile states.

If I am elected and trusted to represent the people of Buckden, Diddington, and Southoe the only promise I can make is that I would use my analytical and communication skills to ensure the interests of all residents are fairly represented. I hold credibility and integrity as the highest trust attributes, and believe that everyone should have a voice, and a say, in the development of services to future proof the delivery of services. We are all to an extent all codependent and achieve what’s in our best interests when we work together.

Finally, please exercise your right to vote, sadly, many people across the world still do not enjoy such a privilege.

I am writing to introduce myself as your Conservative candidate for Fenstanton & Hilton in the forthcoming district council election.

I have lived in Fenstanton for almost 30 years and am an active member of the community, currently Chair of Fenstanton Parish Council and a Parent Governor at our school.

I am a firm believer in working to make things better for all residents, actively taking part in trying to resolve issues and not just complaining from the sidelines.

It was this attitude that lead me to join Fenstanton Parish Council, stand as Chair when needed and become a school governor.

This is the approach that I would like to bring to Huntingdonshire District Council.

I am standing on a platform of accountability, sensible planning, and strong, visible local representation — focused on protecting the character of our villages, ensuring infrastructure keeps pace with development, and improving day-to-day council services.

I hope that on May 7th you will do me the honour of electing me as your representative on the district council.

Thank you for taking the time to read this and please feel free to stop me and ask questions when you see me about in the villages.

Ipswich born Brett is the Liberal Democrat candidate for Central Suffolk and North Ipswich.

He is married, recently celebrating his 25th anniversary, and has two secondary school aged children. He is a district councillor elected two years ago and has cabinet responsibility for Finance and Resources - but he is not a career politician without understanding of life in the real world. He has enjoyed a successful career in technology that has seen him work across multiple industry sectors, seeing both best practice and more recently as a consultant helping companies reverse declines due to poor practice.

Brett is strongly motivated by community and a sense of fairness which is highlighted by his unpaid voluntary work. He was a Scout leader for almost 15 years and qualified as a football referee in 2018 – putting back into activities that he enjoyed so much when he was younger to give the next generation a chance to benefit from them.

He uses his business skills to benefit his County FA as a non-executive director and he was the treasurer of his local church and foodbank for 12 years, stepping back to make time to fight the general election. The foodbank grew from a cupboard in the corner to a vital part of the local community, in 2023 supporting over 500 families and distributing almost 58 tonnes of food as well as securing grants to support town and rural money advice partners to try to give people a hand up not just a hand out.

Brett wants to see a fair deal for everyone that puts real power into people’s hands regardless of wealth or perceived status while holding the already powerful to account.

Mark Turley is first, a husband and a father to his four children and growing number of grandchildren currently six! Mark's career has been in the construction industry having made his way from a day labourer to a director. Mark's career took him to Canada and the United States after being headhunted for his specialist knowledge. Mark has overseen massive building projects like shopping malls and infrastructure. Mark understands what it takes to build a team and bear the weight of decisions, after moving back to the UK in 2010 and becoming a Cambridgeshire resident for the second time in his life Mark threw his full enthusiasm and work ethic into a company he is now the owner and director of  increasing annual turnover by 1000% and growing over the last 15 years. Mark is also a life-long christian who has given faithfully to charities throughout his life and continues to. The ideas of hard work and fair reward run through Mark's business in a culture he has intentionally set. While rewarding hard work Mark also believes it is the duty of the fortunate to give a hand up not a hand out to the less fortunate.

I am proud to put myself forward to represent my fellow residents and seek to be a new voice for Godmanchester, Hemmingford Abbots and the Offords.

As a trained senior administrator, I worked for many years at Cambridge University, before building my own business in 2014 with tenacity, hard work and commitment. I am also proud to have served as a Town Councillor and Deputy Mayor in Godmanchester.   I have lived in the area since 1986 when I moved here from Zimbabwe.

Now, I want to ensure we have an alternative voice on the District Council. Instead of choosing more of the same, lend me your vote and we can take a different path. I will lead the way in lobbying fellow Conservatives for more support and investment in our home area.

This is my home too, the home of my family, my friends and my community, and I am not afraid to speak out to ensure the will of my fellow residents is understood.

A scientist who has spearheaded efforts to improve recycling, waste reduction, environment and healthcare outcomes through product development and strategy. Passionate Labour member for the over 10 years.

I am a Parish Councillor and Data Engineer by Profession, working hard on behalf of the people of this Parish and their wellbeing.
Also as a Council representative in Crimestoppers. working with other security Agencies for stronger and safe communities on the issues of gang’s , thefts and the anti social behaviours. I am also involved in other local community projects such as food bank, community awareness and other Charitable projects.

I’m Stephen Claffey, and I’m standing to be your Liberal Democrat councillor for Great Paxton Ward.

I live in Great Paxton with my family, and I care deeply about protecting village life across our ward, including Great Paxton, Great Gransden, Abbotsley, Waresley, Yelling and Toseland.

I’m not standing to play political games. I’m standing because too many local issues get left to drift, and people are fed up with not knowing who is responsible, what is being done, or whether anyone is properly listening.

As a parish councillor, I’ve already been involved in the kind of practical local work that matters to people, backing road safety improvements, supporting the village shop, helping push local issues forward, and working with residents and local bodies to get things moving.

That is how I believe local politics should work. Listen properly. Be visible. Understand the details. Work hard. Follow through.

If elected to Huntingdonshire District Council, I will focus on the things a good local councillor should focus on: strong representation, clear communication, protecting the character of our villages, supporting local services, and making sure rural communities are not overlooked.

I believe our area deserves a councillor who is rooted here, takes people seriously, and gets stuck into the issues that affect daily life.

If that is the kind of representation you want, I’d be grateful for your support.

I have made Abbotsley my home since 2014, having previously lived in London working as a solicitor. I bring the real-world experience, practical approach, and competence that we sorely need right now.

This is my first time standing for election, and I want to offer a straightforward, practical approach - focused on listening to residents, representing the villages properly, and getting things done.

As a resident of Abbotsley, and having previously served as President of Abbotsley WI, I care deeply about our communities and the people who live here.

I believe in financial prudence, fairness, and protecting the things that make our villages good places to live. Where something needs to be challenged, I will do so - but always with a focus on residents rather than rhetoric.

**After many years of declining services, it can sometimes feel as though little will change for the better. I believe that we can do better, by focusing on local priorities and amplifying residents' voices.**

After the recent Parliamentary win by Hannah Spencer of the Green Party in the Gorton and Denton by-election (26th February 2026) which was started due to the suspension of the sitting MP for sending offensive WhatsApp messages, it became clear to me that we all needed to stand-up and work together to bring back a level of trust, decency, honesty, and fairness to our communities.

Every day we’re bombarded with digital messages, print media, television, radio, and social media messages from other parties like Reform, what’s left of the Conservative party, a Labour party (sadly) in turmoil and disarray, and a Lib Dem party (usually strong in local elections), but falling behind in the polls. The messages are typically divisive, full of fear, division, blame, and hate. Well, to put it bluntly, I’m fed up of the stale, hate filled, blame filled, and (far too often) lies and fictional content that many of the parties that the other candidates are standing for represent, and are sadly not strong enough to call out.

I freely, and without any agenda admit that I don’t know any of the other candidates personally, or even through their political parties they represent, so I can’t and would never comment in any way as to their beliefs, actions, what drives them, what they like, dislike, or what their take is of the present state of the country; Stephen Cawley (Conservative), Ann Christine Fryer (Reform UK), Tony Hulme (Liberal Democrats), or Yavin Michael Owens (Labour), I’m sure, like me, all they all want to do is to make our communities better, offer support, and guidance to those they represent, and above all to be honest, trustworthy, dependable, caring, accountable, and true to their words they use when presenting themselves to the voters.

The present Councilor for the Ward; Stephen Cawley is liked on the Council, his attendance is consistent (always good to see), and I’m sure he’s a decent person who, during his time has represented the Ward well, and has been part o

Dear Neighbours,

I am proud to put myself forward for re-election at this critical time in local government  to continue to deliver and be a strong voice for Hemingford Grey, Houghton & Wyton and Wyton on the Hill.

Born in Huntingdon I grew up in St Ives attending Eastfield Primary School, Westfield Junior School, and the Ivo. As a child, I played football for St Ives Rangers and rugby for St Ives. After studying Business at Plymouth Polytechnic, I built a career in marketing, working in London, the Thames Valley, and across Europe before returning to settle in Houghton & Wyton in 2009.

My connection to this area runs deep. My parents and sister’s family still live in St Ives, and I have extended family in the county, I have a strong love for and commitment to our community.

I have been a Parish Councillor since 2016 and a District Councillor since 2018. I am an active member of our local Timebank, a shareholder in the community shop, and a founding member of the local flood group. These roles have strengthened my understanding of our community’s needs and my resolve to improve it for all residents.

I am asking for your support to continue the strong record of delviery for residents at distric level well trying to reduce unnecessary bureaucracy, and ensuring your hard earned tax money delivers value where it matters most.

I will also stand firm against overdevelopment, push for better public transport, and protect our unique environment. This is my home too, and I am committed to making sure your voice is heard and acted upon.

If you have concerns or ideas, please don’t hesitate to reach out. Together, we can build a brighter, stronger future for our community.

Yours David

Promoted by Ross Martin Bergamont Close, Godmanchester, PE29 2LY, on behalf of HCCA,

I am proud to stand with Cllr David Keane for election at this important time for Huntingdonshire.

I have lived in the District all my life, the last 20 years within this ward. I have served as a school governor and a Parish Councillor and have set up a Neighbourhood Watch scheme.

I will push back against development that does not come with infrastructure and facilities, strive for better public transport, and protect the identity of our individual communities.

Together, we can build a brighter, stronger future for our community.

Thank you for your support.

Paul

Promoted by Ross Martin Bergamont Close, Godmanchester, PE29 2LY, on behalf of HCCA,

David has lived with his family in Hemingford Grey for 14 years. He adores the village and surrounding areas for their beauty, serenity, quality of life and, above all, the warmth and good character of the community. He expects to live here for many more happy years. Having served on Huntingdonshire District Council from 2006-2010 when he lived in Huntingdon, he would like the chance to represent the people of the Hemingfords, Fenstanton, Hilton and Houghton, articulating and defending their concerns on environmental, planning, roads and other key local issues.

He has run a local media company for nearly 30 years, including publishing a market-leading logistics magazine and reporting globally on a crucial industrial sector. As an employer he understands the importance of economic issues in Cambridgeshire, which are sometimes overlooked and he brings a rational, impartial and problem-solving approach to all matters.

David holds degrees in both history and philosophy and, via his 3 children, knows the local schools and how central they remain to our community. His interests include kayaking on the River Ouse every week between St Ives and Godmanchester, which constantly reminds him of the need to protect our precious natural environment. A lifelong liberal, David’s political views are inclusive, positive and based on a desire to create a truly meritocratic society.

“I’m a recently retired primary school teacher and have lived and worked in Huntingdonshire since 2011. I’ve had a passion for animals since childhood and am a firm believer in the benefits of whole food plant-based diets for people, planet and animals. In my free time, I love taking care of my three grandchildren, walking with my rescued German Shepherd and relaxing in the garden with my six rescued chickens. If elected, I’ll strive to be a strong voice for all who call this beautiful area home - humans and animals alike.

If elected on 7th May, my priorities include:

Ensuring council support for a swift transition to animal-free science (Herbie’s Law) thereby releasing the district from being associated with outdated animal testing, as highlighted by Camp Beagle’s five year long permanent presence in the ward

Protecting local wildlife habitats and river meadows

Tree planting to protect against and mediate risks of projected flooding in the region. (Climate Central’s 2030 Flood Map shows an area projected to be below annual flood level from The Wash to Fen Drayton Lakes)

Initiating a council ‘Eco Hub’ to promote and advise on sustainable living

Improving cycle routes and footpaths, to ensure year-round usability

Mandating wildlife tunnels or native hedging for all boundaries in new planning applications

Ensuring the River Great Ouse is healthy and pollution-free, via a local Charter to protect its rights

Preparing our children to live sustainable lifestyles, including support for schools to promote plant-based options and cookery lessons

Advocating for plant-based food and drink options to be served in public settings, including council-catered events

Opposing new planning applications for intensive farming or related processing in the area."

I have lived in Godmanchester for the past 15 years. I am a former Town Councillor and a active local campaigner and a community champion. I will fight for your rights, I have a burning desire to be a councillor and love to serve Community. I hope you will encourage me and elect me as a counsellor

Cllr Patrick Kadewere – Labour Candidate | Huntingdon North
Cllr Patrick Kadewere is a dedicated community leader who has proudly served Huntingdon since 2010 as a Town Councillor and since 2012 as a District Councillor for Huntingdon North. He currently serves as Deputy Mayor of Huntingdon (2025–2026), representing the town at civic events and championing community unity.
Patrick has built a reputation for being a hands-on, results-driven councillor, focused on delivering real improvements for residents. His work has included campaigning for better road conditions and accountability on highways, securing long-overdue footpath resurfacing, supporting housing cases, and strengthening partnerships that help residents through the cost-of-living crisis.
Beyond the Council, Patrick is deeply embedded in the community. He leads the Oxmoor Community Fridge initiative, helping reduce food waste while supporting families, and works closely with local organisations, faith groups, and charities to bring people together. He is also actively involved in youth development through his work with Police Cadets, helping to guide and inspire the next generation.
Professionally experienced outside politics, Patrick brings practical understanding, compassion, and strong leadership to his role. He is committed to listening to residents, standing up for their concerns, and ensuring their voices are heard in decision-making.
Patrick believes in unity, respect, and action — working across communities and political lines to deliver the best outcomes for Huntingdon.

I moved to Huntingdon three years ago when my husband was posted to RAF Wyton. After years of moving around, it’s been a real pleasure to finally put down roots, build a home, and become part of this community.

I’m standing because Huntingdon North deserves better from its district council.
The Conservative plan for Huntingdon North is simple:

- Stop the Lib Dem council tax rises that have hit household budgets while local services are stretched or cut
- Stand up for Huntingdon North, making sure our area gets its fair share of attention and investment from the district council
- Back hardworking families, keeping council tax as low as possible so people can keep more of what they earn

Times are tough enough already. Residents deserve a council that focuses on priorities, value for money, and putting local people first.

Labour group leader in cabinet on Huntingdonshire district council, leading on creating more affordable homes, better connected public transport and improving job opportunities for local people. Professional experience in education and health policy, and representing NHS staff to improve their working conditions in the NHS.

I'm proud to represent the Green Party at the ballot box and help fight for a fairer, greener future for everyone. I've lived in Huntingdonshire for almost all of my life, and I love it here - I'm grateful to have this opportunity to give back to my community and help make our area the best it can be. I'm an experienced Councillor, representing Huntingdon West, and I was delighted to be recognised as one of the Green Party's '30 under 30' this year. My main priority is to help support people through this cost of living crisis - the Government has failed to support residents through this crisis, and the ordinary public are paying the price. We're also seeing the first signs of the climate crisis on our doorstep, with record flooding over the past few years turning lives upside down. Our area's biodiversity is also collapsing - with huge parts of our wildlife at risk of being lost forever. Alongside so many people, I'm frustrated by the lack of reliable public transport and active travel infrastructure in our towns and villages. We live in an area which relies on residents owning a car to access basic services like shops and GPs. If elected, I'll work to ensure that everyone, especially people in rural areas, has access to reliable public transport and proper walking and cycling routes. Huntingdon is also an expensive place to rent or buy a place to live, with people of all ages priced out of our area. I'm campaigning to make the rental market fairer, including calling for an end to no-fault evictions, and more support to get local people onto the housing ladder - by boosting housing stock through a mix of refurbishment, conversions, and suitable new builds.

Keith Prentice is currently a County Councillor in St Neots who switched from the Conservatives. He has plenty of experience having also been an Executive Councillor for leisure and regulatory services in Huntingdonshire District.

The 11 Villages of the Kimbolton Ward are special places to live here in West Huntingdonshire: each has its own distinctive community spirit.

But things are not going to stay the same. We know that Local Government Reform will create a new Council, whilst the Labour Government seems intend on allowing housing and solar farms wherever developers want.

Jonathan Gray was born and bred in Cambridgeshire and Kimbolton resident for almost 30 years. He combines an insurance business career in with serving as a school governor and District Councillor. He has represented Kimbolton at the District Council for 22 years, serving in the Council's Cabinet for 14 years, and as Leader of the Opposition between 2022 and 2024.

Jonathan provides a strong and experienced voice for Kimbolton in this time of change, making sure that the values and concerns of rural communities are not ignored.

His priorities are:

1. Stopping Inappropriate Housing Developments, and making sure that those that are approved have the correct infrastructure. For example, his lobbying has led to 2 housing developments near Kimbolton being paused to find workable solutions to prevent sewage entering the River Kym.

2. Ensuring that proper infrastructure is provided for any development and that our existing infrastructure is better maintained. Jonathan has been working closely with our County Councillor Ian Gardener to tackle the blight of potholes that 5 years of a LibDem County Council has led to.

3. End the Green Bin Tax Madness. We had a perfectly workable solution for collecting garden and food waste. But the foolish decision of the Joint Administration (all parties plus independents, other than Conservative) to charge for green waste has seen thousands of tons of food waste go to landfill, and also means buying 10's of thousands of food caddies to now collect food waste that is, predictably, mandated by Westminster.

4. Keeping more money in your pocket. Between 2013 and 2022 I was responsible for HD

I started working on the farm aged 8, and have worked multiple jobs and juggled my own business and a family all at the same time, relentlessly my whole life! I am a real person, have an unmatched work ethic and it would be a pleasure to use it to serve my homelands.  Helping put all the experience gained over the years in to serving as your Mayor.

If you have followed me for a while, seen my work across the county, you will know I mean business for Cambridgeshire and Peterborough, for those who have not, it is my absolute pleasure to show what can be done from outside the 'club' so just imagine what can be achieved with a vote to place me inside!

I live in Ramsey. I love the area, the community and friendly people. I am a lawyer, so used to holding people to account and ensuring fairness and justice for all. I'm standing for ReformUK in Ramsey because we have been let down by successive governments, locally and nationally.
If elected, I want to understand, scrutinise, challenge and review the current working practices of HDC to deliver improvements for local people through common sense.
With your vote, I will represent your voice at local  government level to make sure the council is accountable to you the electorate. 
The main 3 priorities coming from the doorsteps are:
1. Fix the roads 
2. Safer streets
3. Support small businesses
A vote for all Reform candidates is a vote for a chance at a better future for all in Ramsey and Huntingdonshire.

I am pleased to announce that I will be standing for re-election in the Cambridgeshire County Elections this May 2025. Serving as a County Councillor since 2013 has been an honour, and I remain dedicated to representing our community with pride, determination, and integrity.
This year marks 26 years since I made this community my home. Over that time, I’ve built a strong network of people, partners, charities, and stakeholders, allowing me to collaborate effectively and find practical solutions to local challenges.
As a former Royal Marine, Police officer & business owner, I am
passionate about low taxation and efficient service delivery, ensuring residents get the best value for their money.
Over the past four years, I have stood up for residents as part of theopposition party at the County Council. Despite challenges, including the poor state of our highways, I successfully helped reverse the misguided no-weed policy. I was also proud to play a key role in helping deliver Sawtry’s new 3G pitch, working with local stakeholders to secure funding and ensure this vital facility became a reality for residents and sports clubs alike.
Looking ahead, I will continue to ensure your concerns are heard,
especially when we face large-scale development proposals. It’s vital we have an experienced voice to challenge unnecessary projects and secure the infrastructure required to support any that proceed.
Thank you for trusting me to serve the Sawtry and Stilton Ward. I
encourage everyone to ensure they go out and vote on 1st May 2025 to re-elect me as your local champion.

**Dedication to Sawtry**
As a Sawtry resident for 21 years, my dedication to our community, passion for making a positive impact, and drive to ensure our village thrives is at the heart of everything I do. Having attended local schools and recently bought my first home here, I am deeply invested in our village's future.

**A Proven Track Record**
I have a proven track record of delivering results for our area as Sawtry's proven voice. I don’t just talk—I take action. Together with the community, we successfully saved the Sawtry Swimming Pool and got it back open, and I have actively fought against speculative development.

**Crucial Elections for Our Future**
This year’s elections are more crucial than ever. Your councillor won’t just need to tackle our crumbling roads and bursting GP surgery by demanding an "infrastructure first" approach—they will also play a key role in shaping our community through challenges like local government reorganisation.

**Experience You Can Trust**
Combining my experience as a business owner, Councillor, and Leader of the Opposition, I know how to navigate the system to deliver for Sawtry. Working daily as the right-hand man to our MP, Ben Obese-Jecty, I am well-equipped to achieve our shared vision.

**Holding the Council Accountable & Keeping More Money in Your Pocket**
Like many of you, I have watched the current Lib Dem-led HDC make poor decisions, such as waving through development on the "Land South of Sawtry" without even requiring an Environmental Impact Assessment.

Furthermore, I am committed to keeping more money in your pocket. Every penny the Council holds is your money, and while other parties demand more of your hard-earned cash, I believe no-one knows how to spend your money better than you do. That is why we proposed freezing Council Tax this year, and it will be the default if we take control.

**My Plan for Sawtry**
Now more than ever, we need a strong, experienced Conservative voice to sit at the top table and figh

St Ives has been my home for my entire life, and I owe so much to it. Having attended school and worked in electronics here, I am now a soon-to-be history graduate. As a young man who aspires to start a family here, I am determined to invest in our future and make our community the strongest and safest it can be.

I am committed to delivering the change that residents deserve:

1. Eliminate waste and ensure that residents get the best possible services for the council tax they pay. 
2. Support local businesses and revitalise our town centre.
3. Take a considerate approach to planning, ensuring that new development does not strain our infrastructure.
4. Ensure that local people are first on the list for housing.
5. Work with the County Council to fix the roads, support our police, improve street lighting, and bolster flood defences to ensure that we live in a safe and secure community.

Getting the basics rights - an active, visible and committed Councillor for St Ives East.

Hi, I'm Luke. I've been an active member of our community since moving to Huntingdonshire - attending civic events, supporting local charities, and doorknocking around St Ives to hear your concerns.

**My pledges for St Ives East**

➡️ **Active, visible and committed.** I will canvass all year round, not only for elections. I’ll hold regular, well advertised surgeries in local venues for all.
➡️ **Championing  St Ives community groups.** I’ll be a District Councillor who shows up at community events and pushes for local organisation funding.
➡️ **Pride in St Ives.** Working with the Labour Government, I will fight for fair funding for our local councils so St Ives gets the resources it needs.
➡️ **Supporting affordable housing with new infrastructure.** I’ll work with the council to deliver affordable housing with the much needed infrastructure in first.
➡️ **Getting the basics right.** No issue is too small - from reporting potholes to weeds, I will be responsive and ensure residents get value for their council tax.
➡️ **Keeping our council local in the new unitaries.** Like all Labour District and County Councillors and candidates in Huntingdonshire, I supported Option E for an independent Huntingdonshire Council and campaigned against the Liberal Democrat County Council's preferred option to merge us with Fenland and Peterborough.

Some parties make appealing promises in local elections -  such as "fixing the NHS" or changing immigration policy. This is a local district council election, not an election for Members of Parliament. The question to ask is - who would be an active and committed local representative? Who would keep to the values they were elected to represent?

Unlike the 2022 elections, all parties are standing in St Ives East. Every vote will truly matter.

**Please vote for me, Luke Viner, on the 7th of May.**

Active, visible and committed - getting the job done for St Ives South.

Hello, I'm Alex - many of you will know me as your County Councillor for St Ives South. This is the last election for Huntingdonshire District Council before the new unitary councils in 2027-2028. I am standing for Huntingdonshire District Council in St Ives South as I believe we need experienced councillors in this important reorganisation phase - as well as continue to represent your priorities locally.

I won in May 2025 the county council seat that includes St Ives - the first time Labour won this county seat since the seat was created! I am grateful to everyone who has put their trust in me and it has been a privilege to represent St Ives South residents over the last year. I would like to be an active and strong voice for you on the District Council as well.

**My top 3 pledges**
➡️ Doorknocking all year round and holding monthly in-person surgeries 
➡️ Ensuring development is done properly - with infrastructure in first and not on sites that increase flood risk
➡️ Keeping our council local and preserving our Huntingdonshire identity in the new unitary councils

**I have a strong track record on the County Council, and I will do the same on the District**
✅ **Present.** Monthly surgeries for residents at the library and all year-round canvassing listening to your concerns and following up with actions
✅ **Proactive.** Vocal in every council meeting, whether it's standing up against the County Lib Dem's proposal to merge us with Fenland and Peterborough in the new unitaries, or speaking up on local issues such as flooding and busway safety
✅ **Principled.** Councillors should be the voice of residents on the council, not the other way round. I have been an effective opposition member, and I challenge in a constructive way.

You may hear promises on other leaflets that sound appealing but simply cannot be delivered at a local level - such as "fixing the NHS" or changing immigration policy..

My name is Will. I am a teacher at a local school and am passionate about education, opportunities for young people (as one of the younger candidates myself) and bringing more investment into the town. I am standing for the first time because I want people across our community to feel represented and listened to.

My key pledges:
- Doorknocking all year round and holding monthly in-person surgeries.
- Ensuring development is done properly - with infrastructure in first and not on sites that increase flood risk.
- Keeping our council local and preserving our identity in the new unitary councils.

Dear Neighbour,

I am honoured to stand as your Conservative candidate for the St Ives South and Needingworth division. As someone deeply rooted in our community, I’m passionate about representing our shared interests and ensuring our voices are heard.

I’ve lived in St Ives for many years, raising my family here and seeing first-hand what makes our area special. I’ve served as a district councillor for Huntingdonshire District Council and as a Town Councillor for St Ives, where I was also privileged to serve as Deputy Mayor. These roles allowed me to contribute to our community’s growth and address local concerns.

Professionally, I’m an electrical hospital engineer at Addenbrooke’s, managing teams that deliver fast and effective solutions. My career is built on hard work, problem-solving, and collaboration – skills I’ll bring to the County Council.

Our area deserves a councillor who will fight for better infrastructure, improved roads, and enhanced support for schools and social care. I’ll also champion investment in skills and training opportunities, revitalise our town centres, and protect our community from overdevelopment.

This is my home – where my family, friends, and neighbours live. I will work tirelessly to deliver practical, positive change for our area.

If you have any concerns or ideas on how I can serve our community, please don’t hesitate to get in touch.

Best regards,

Craig Smith
Conservative County Council Candidate for St Ives South and Needingworth

I was privileged to be elected for St Ives South  in 2022
The last 4 years have been eventful with many  firsts.   I was appointed chair of Overview & Scrutiny , Performance & Growth panel and waa a member of the  Local Plan Group.
I have lived in St Ives for 30 years  and am a Director of St Ives Corn Exchange 
I have 2 grand children who live  locally and look after on a regular basis. 
I  have worked in social care as a commissioner and have also run my own business as a change manager

Passionate about St Ives because I live in St Ives.

A pharmacist for the NHS and a family man who listens to your issues and cares too. No empty promises, real answers to your real issues.

As a town councillor already, I serve and provide a sound voice for you, my neighbours. As a member of one of our churches, I will continue to serve our community to the best of my ability in an honest way.

On election day (7th May) vote for a sensible candidate who stands united with you. Please vote for me, Andrew Riddell, your conservative party candidate.

Lara has lived in the area since 2013 and was elected to Huntingdonshire District Council in 2022. She currently leads the Council’s work on Climate and Environment.

Lara is also a school governor, a community champion and recently completed three years as chair of Love’s Farm Community Association. Lara:
* works hard all year
* listens to your views
* is an independent voice for Love's Farm and Wintringham

Lara's Priorities
* A rail station for Wintringham on the new East-West Rail line
* Warm homes, built to high energy efficiency standards
* Affordable homes, built in the right places
* Maximising rooftop solar energy generation
* Thriving high streets in your vibrant villages and market towns
* Advance prep of school places - reflecting changing populations
* Safe roads
* Safe pavements
* Safe rural rights of way for people, cycles and horse riders
* Positive and respectful local politics - improving public trust

Your Choice on 7th May
* Lara works hard all year round, not just at election time
* Lara is the Cabinet member for Climate & Environment on Huntingdonshire District Council
* Greens aren’t told what to do or how to vote by Party leaders in Westminster

Stephen has lived in St Neots East for 12 years and has served the town as a Town, County and District Councillor. He was twice elected Mayor of St Neots and currently serves as a Cabinet member on Huntingdonshire District Council.

Stephen leads the Council’s work on housing, benefits, anti-social behaviour prevention and licensing. His role focuses on protecting vulnerable residents, supporting families, and keeping communities safe.

Stephen:
• works hard all year round
• listens to residents and acts on what matters
• stands up for fairness and social justice

Stephen’s Priorities

• Making sure families who need homes can get them
• Tackling anti-social behaviour and keeping neighbourhoods safe
• Fair and responsible taxation that protects those on the lowest incomes
• Supporting residents through the benefits system with dignity and respect
• Strong local services that work for everyday people
• Practical action to support vulnerable households

Your Choice on 7th May

• Stephen has a proven record of service to St Neots
• Stephen has led real work on housing, benefits and community safety
• Stephen will continue to stand up for fairness, responsibility and local people

Dr Geoff Seeff moved from London to St Neots for family reasons during the pandemic lockdown in 2020. He has been an active Liberal Democrat for over 40 years and has stood as a parliamentary candidate on several occasions, most recently in 2019 in Chingford and Woodford Green. A chartered accountant and management consultant, his doctoral research was in the field of corporate social responsibility. Following a secondment to the Department of the Environment, his career has been focused on urban regeneration and he was up until July 2025 employed with McBains, an international technical services practice, as an assessor and monitor of social infrastructure and commercial construction projects on behalf of both private and public investors and funders. He was formerly the Chairman of the Woodford Festival, a community festival of visual and performing arts, and is himself an enthusiastic, albeit a less than virtuoso, jazz musician. He swims regularly and has participated in numerous fund raising swim events.
He was first elected as the County Councillor for St Neots the Eatons in the by-election of February 2023 and re-elected in the local elections held in May 2025. He has been responsible for several initiatives including pressing for information on allergens to be displayed prominently in outlets serving non-packaged foods so that customers do not have to ask. He also identified a £1 million of previously uncollected S106 funds that have now been applied for highway improvements and education projects.
He is currently Chair of the Cambridgeshire Pension Fund, Chair of the Library Presents and sits on the Music Services board and the Communities and Social Inclusion Committee.

Has lived in Cambridgeshire all of his life. Wants communities to have the right infrastructure. Michael’s particular focus will be a better bus service - he’s spent far too long at stops waiting for buses which don’t turn up.

I’m standing as a Party of Women candidate for St Neots and Mid Cambridgeshire because I do not believe anybody on the ballot paper will adequately represent me, an adult human female.

I initially became aware of the issue of ‘transitioning’ around 2011 while working within Littlehey Prison. I left the prison thinking ‘this is mad, nobody will allow men into women’s prisons!’ I pushed my concerns aside knowing ‘there is nothing I can do’, after an incident in 2017 I lifted the rock, began reading, found and joined women’s groups, read more, attended events, listened and eventually started writing letters, then getting involved with on street activism.

Despite living in a democracy, over the years my questions to elected representatives about aspects of self-identification (self ID), gender identity and the impact on women and girls, have been ignored, met with derision, scorn and name calling, rather than reasoned conversation.

I live in Eynesbury with my husband and our dogs, and I am standing as an Independent candidate for St Neots Town Council and Huntingdonshire District Council because I believe councillors should live in and truly understand the communities they represent. With 27 years’ experience working in local government at both District and County level, and now working for a national charity supporting councils across the country, I understand how councils work and how to ensure local voices are heard and taken seriously.

Why This Election Matters
This year is an important one for local democracy. Alongside the Town Council elections, residents will also be voting in the upcoming Huntingdonshire District Council election, at a time when we are waiting for a decision on which Local Government Reform (LGR) option will be chosen for our area. These changes could shape how services are delivered and how decisions affecting our area are made for many years to come. I believe it is more important than ever that St Neots and Eynesbury have strong, independent voices at Town Council and District level, working constructively with County councillors to stand up for our community.

An Independent Voice for Eynesbury
I am standing as an Independent because I have no party political agenda. My focus is on:
•	Representing the best interests of Eynesbury residents
•	Listening to your concerns
•	Speaking up for your priorities
•	Focusing on practical, deliverable outcomes

Experience and Local Involvement
Over the past two years, I have been closely involved in shaping decisions that affect our area. I chair the Multi Parish Neighbourhood Plan Steering Group, serve as Vice Chair of the Council’s Environment & Emergency Committee, have contributed to the District Council’s Local Plan. I am also on the working group that reviews proposed changes to our streets (Local Highways Improvements), always focused on practical, deliverable outcomes for Eynesbury.

Protecting Green Spaces
One issue I

Deanna and her husband Geoff moved from Woodford Essex to St Neots over 6 years ago to be with their daugher and her family. She is an accredited psycotherapist with an established local practice and who, before her move, was also a school counsellor and a school governor. She is currently a member of the St Neots Health Centre Patients' Participation Group. She was born in Istanbul, travelled extensively with her parents whilst young and as a result is fluent in four languages. A founder member of the SDP in 1981  and a Liberal Democrat since, her principal political interests are focused on health and mental; health, social welfare and education
Hard working Town Councillor and Chairty chair, keen to step up and help the community of Eynesbury and the wider St Neots area.

On May 7th, I am standing as an independent candidate in the Eynesbury ward for both the Town Council and District Council.

I’ve lived in St Neots and have grown up here since I was 5. In the past few years I have volunteered as part of our St Neots Youth Council, working on ways in which support for young people can be improved in the town.

Examples of some of this work include:

- Helping to run youth events and services across the town 
- Carrying out Intergenerational projects such as at Henbrook House
- Advocating for and recently helping to secure funding for a dedicated Youth Building in the town

As an independent my decisions will be shaped by the people that I represent not by the views of those in Westminster. My focus will be on representing the best interests of Eynesbury residents, listening to your concerns and speaking up for the things that are actually important to you.

As a town and district councillor I will be:

✅ Supporting ‘Option E’ of local government reform that gives Huntingdonshire and St Neots a stronger voice with its own council, as opposed to being combined with Peterborough, which could lead to higher council tax and worse outcomes for our residents  
✅ A leading voice in the delivery of the designated Youth Building in St Neots
✅ Advocating for a ‘St Neots East’ station on the East-West Rail line

As well and working with the County Council to:

✅ Deal with parking issues in Eynesbury 
✅ Fix the potholes plaguing our drivers

Ben

wards.ward: St Neots Priory Park & Little Paxton

candidates.stmt:
I’m Richard Tomlinson, your Conservative candidate for Huntingdonshire District Council in St Neots Priory Park & Little Paxton. I live in Little Paxton and currently serve as Chair of the Parish Council.

Over the past few years I’ve been focused on practical local issues — from road safety, speeding and crossings to parking pressures and the condition of our roads. I’ve reported hundreds of potholes locally, and worked with the council to secure improvements where possible.

I’ve also been championing work to find long-term solutions to flooding at Mill Lane Bridge — pushing for both immediate fixes and a proper long-term plan.

I believe your councillors should show up and do the work. I have a 100% attendance record at Little Paxton Parish Council, and I regularly attend St Neots Town Council, Huntingdonshire District Council and Cambridgeshire County Council meetings to speak up for local residents.

My focus is simple: getting things done for St Neots and Little Paxton.  I've been doing that in my parish council role and would continue to do that if elected to district council.

I previously worked as a director at an international law firm, Chief Knowledge Officer in financial services, and now run my own consulting business, helping organisations improve how they operate and deliver results.

If you have not yet met me but have either questions or need assistance with any Council matter then please contact me and I will always help. I’ve been a Councillor in the Eatons for nearly two decades, my professional expertise is in management of large projects and before I became a Councillor I volunteered for ten years at Citizens Advice, with debt work and the homeless, often assisting local people facing serious problems. I have been elected Mayor of St Neots three times and have served in other years as the Town’s Deputy Mayor. I have experience as a District Councillor and County Councillor and have served on Cambridgeshire & Peterborough Combined Authority but most relevant is having already helped many hundreds of local residents with Council issues. As a long standing member of the Eatons Community Association you may have seen me cooking at the May Day barbeque, at Christmas Carols on Eaton Socon Green or other events which raise money to support Eatons charities and community groups. I am also President of the Choral Society, which has regular concerts in St Mary’s Eaton Socon and may have met you there too or perhaps during a litter pick or when planting roadside daffodils or inviting passers by to view the Lock-up near Jubilee Hall! My wife and I have lived in the Eatons since 1978 and our children attended school with children of many other families here. Throughout my life I’ve watched our area grow and understand the need to deliver improvements to match that growth whilst preserving our community identity and heritage. I am genuinely passionate about where we live and totally dedicated to improving things, helping residents and getting more for our area. As an Independent candidate I don’t have political party funding for endless leaflets and would ask you to share this with friends and family. It contains just some of the things which I have already achieved for our community and some, which I am hoping you will give me the opportunity to comple

I'm Freya, one of two Labour candidates for The Stukeleys: I'm running as I'm from Stukeley, for Stukeley.

I believe we need more young people involved in the decisions that will affect our local area. This is where I grew up and it's my home.

I'm running with Sikohnzile Ndebele and our promises to local residents are:
**1) Present & Proactive**
We will engage year-round in our local area and be visible in person and online for residents.

**2) Getting things done**
We will support the District Council in getting the basics right. This means bins collected, parks kept clean, and residents put first on issues that matter to them!

**3) Transparency**
Any decision we engage in will be communicated to residents.

For candidates who will go out, get things done and listen to residents, vote Labour this May.

I have absolutely loved serving as the District Councillor for Warboys Ward over the past four years. It has been a privilege to represent our villages, support residents and stand up for the issues that matter most.  As Huntingdonshire District Council prepares for the transition to a Unitary Authority in the coming years, I hope to continue representing you and making sure our communities are heard.

Becoming a District Councillor wasn’t something I had planned.  Someone asked me if I’d consider it and I thought, “Why not?”  I’ve always been driven by a genuine desire to help people - and this role has allowed me to do exactly that.  Whether supporting flood‑hit communities, backing the new Warboys Community Centre, or pushing for better roads, transport and planning decisions, I’ve worked hard to make sure local people see real, practical benefits.

My passion for our community is what keeps me going.  I want our villages to have the investment, services and representation they deserve.  For the past four years, Conservative Councillors have been in opposition, holding the Joint Administration to account - including Calling‑In the Green Bin Subscription Service (or as many residents call it, the Green Bin Tax).  We raised concerns early on, especially with food waste collections on the horizon, but those concerns were not listened to.

Pidley has been my home for 15 years and I’ve dedicated myself to our community in every way I can.  I’ve served as a Trustee for Pidley Mountain Rescue, Chairman of Pidley‑cum‑Fenton Parish Council and a Trustee for the Community Centre.  Every role has strengthened my belief that local people deserve strong, practical, hands‑on representation.

I will always fight for what is right and do everything I can to make a positive difference in people’s lives.

I understand the frustrations many feel with national politics.  District Councillors are different - we live in the communities we represent, we listen and we work hard for our

# A Voice for Our Community

After 35 years of calling Huntingdonshire home, I understand what matters to our community. As a mother of five children, I've experienced firsthand the joys and challenges of raising a family here. My professional background includes time as an accountant, though my most important roles have been as a stay-at-home mum and now as a full-time carer for my husband, which has given me a deep understanding of the challenges many families face in accessing support services.

If elected to represent Warboys, I will tackle the many issues voters have shared with me over the last few weeks, including: HGVs using Warboys' High Street, hazardous pavements, speeding traffic through Pidley, unresolved drain problems and consequent flooding, a lack of facilities for families in smaller villages and considering the affect on residents of future development in the area.

I'll also work diligently to address rural crime and security concerns across the community.

My commitment is simple: to be your voice, to listen, and to act on the issues that affect your daily lives.

This pandemic year has taught us all the importance of our immediate environment. As a mother of four children who  lives in a Huntingdonshire village, I am well aware of those things that need to be done to improve the quality of life in the villages where we now spend far more time than previously

Children and parents should have access to cycleways so they can travel independently to school or shops and to safe crossings so they can get to local parks where they can enjoy the freedom of playing together.

Rural bus services should be improved so that older people can get out to shops or meet friends. There should be accessible cashpoints in every village.

These are the things I will strive to bring about if I am elected.

Councils are going bust, the NHS and social care are in crisis, homes in Cambridgeshire are way beyond the reach of ordinary people and homelessness has doubled. Young people are forced into crippling dept or onto meaningless poverty paid apprenticeships. Work and state pensions cannot maintain any sort of living and the retired are forced into work until they drop. The Tories have outsourced their destructive austerity and local council’s budgets have been reduced by 40% or more. The government is paralysed by Brexit and have neither the energy nor the will to address today's problems. And there is to be no end as the Tories have convinced us that this is all inevitable, no choice, suffer for a bit longer, then a bit more. With the self-destruction of UKIP there is a local opportunity to put an alternative view onto the Tory dominated local council. Vote Labour and I will represent ordinary people and put public services and jobs first for Ramsey.
